adversarial.description: Beyond re-running all 8 contract verifications to EXIT:0: (E01) anti-vacuity confirms that at HEAD~1 (the F-055 merge 15a30e1) the template still held the {{TIER}} placeholder and did NOT validate against the schema, and no F-054 guard existed; (E03) the conformance guard is proven load-bearing (non-vacuous) -- restoring a {{TIER}} placeholder makes jsonschema.validate FAIL, so the guard would catch a real regression rather than pass on any parseable JSON; (E04) the three surfaces are checked for positive agreement AND for the absence of the stale parse-validity-only caveats in both READMEs. Option A was chosen over B precisely so the mechanical assertion is non-vacuous (schema-validity), not a bare parse check.
adversarial.process_note: Single-session collapsed-role execution, disclosed-and-permitted by the AGF Orchestrator clause. E06 dogfoods the NDEBT-019 rc-capture rule. The load-bearing probe (E03) mutates the template only in memory; the real tree is never mutated.

evidence_bundle_check.diff_patch_reasoning: .agent/evidence/054/diff.patch is `git diff HEAD~1..HEAD` at deliverable commit ef58512; E07 independently re-asserts HEAD~1..HEAD touches exactly the six contracted files and no .agent path.

acceptance_reconciliation.summary: The single acceptance test -- 'the chosen alignment is implemented and the three surfaces (template, schema description, templates/README.md) agree; the template's contract is mechanically asserted' -- maps to: alignment implemented -> E02 (template validates end-to-end); three surfaces agree -> E04 (schema description now true; both READMEs' parse-validity-only caveats retired -- schema/README.md checked too, a fourth surface); mechanically asserted -> E05 (the fixtures_self_test guard reports OK) + E03 (the guard is load-bearing, not vacuous). Option A (schema-valid template) was chosen so the mechanical assertion is meaningful.

non_blocking_observations: SCOPE OVER ESTIMATE (disclosed): 69 actual ins+del vs 40 estimated (+73%); small in absolute terms. The overage is the two governed-README version-bump + change_log entries C8 requires when their caveats are reconciled (templates/README.md 0.2.2, schema/README.md 0.7.1). scope_over_estimate flag, NOT the 3x-rolling flag (last-3 avg 052=678/053=221/055=282 = 394, 3x=1181; 69 far under); cumulative 1763/2236 = 78.8%.

CUMULATIVE SECTION-6 GATE APPROACHING (flagged for the operator): after 054 the phase stands at 1763/2236 (78.8% of the 130% ceiling). The remaining ungated roots -- 056 (est 260), 057 (est 80) -- plus the gated 058 (est 180) and 059 (phase close), with typical overrun, will approach or breach the ceiling. Per methodology/00_planning.md Section 6 the cumulative check is a HARD halt requiring explicit operator re-baseline authorization; it will surface when 056 is taken up. 054 was chosen as the small, budget-preserving next step so the bigger 056 can be sequenced after that decision.

NEXT-FEATURE CHOICE was abort-driven (disclosed): the AskUserQuestion selecting the next root aborted (permission stream closed, as before 050/053/055); proceeded with 054 under standing authorization + the merge-as-endorsement of #33, PR as the redirect mechanism.

PR #34 REVIEW ROUND (disclosed, addressed): CodeRabbit (CHANGES_REQUESTED) raised two findings, both fixed in b3d3de1. (Major) tier-0/local/0 shipped as literal template defaults would silently persist in a copied packet (the workflow only substitutes {{...}} tokens) and misclassify it -- resolved by OMITTING the three optional enum/integer dispatch fields from the starter template (documented in the schema), the framework-aligned mechanical resolution over a doc-warning; the template still validates end-to-end and the guard is unchanged. (Minor) the NDEBT-011 'PR #TBD' placeholder filled with #34. E02 now asserts the omission; the verdict is unchanged (8/8) -- the fix removes a consumer-facing footgun while preserving the feature's goal.

final_verdict.summary: Feature 054 APPROVED. All 8 contract verifications re-run to EXIT:0. NDEBT-011 is resolved via Option A: templates/work-packet.template.json now validates end-to-end against schema/work-packet.schema.json (tier-0/local/0 in the three formerly-placeholder fields), so the schema's own description claim is true; templates/README.md 0.2.2 and schema/README.md 0.7.1 retire the parse-validity-only caveat; and a tools/fixtures_self_test.sh guard mechanically asserts the conformance in CI (proven load-bearing by E03, so non-vacuous). Self-test 46/46, validate 13/13, payload 11/11, e2e green. DEBT 0.25.0. Three disclosed non-blocking items: the scope-over-estimate flag, the approaching Section-6 cumulative gate, and the abort-driven feature choice.
